title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Behavior Case: Media Alternatives
summary: Templates emit multiple mains, heading levels follow visual size, skip links target missing IDs, repeated links have vague labels, code lacks context, and media alternatives are never reviewed. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the media alternatives increment in WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Media Alternatives · Theory Media Alternatives
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 9994 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Media Alternatives contract for the supplied data.

Every image should have a detailed nonempty alt attribute. That claim fails the predict media alternatives evidence boundary for Media Alternatives.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-media-alternatives — Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-accessibility-semantics-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_accessibility_semantics_5(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-media-alternatives-7qx23f
# Competency: ssg-media-alternatives.
# Case 6934: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_media_alternatives_7qx23f(pages: int = 8, checked_pages: int = 8, structural_findings: int = 0, media_decisions_missing: int = 0, human_tasks_passed: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_7qx23f = "7qx23f-f32xq7-6934"
    assert evidence_variant_7qx23f
    evidence_axes_7qx23f = (
        "reader7qx23f",
        "source6934",
        "parsef32xq7",
        "route7qx23f6934",
        "artifact69347qx23f",
        "recoveryf32xq76934",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_7qx23f)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or checked_pages < 0:
        return False, checked_pages, "page-count"
    if checked_pages != pages:
        return False, checked_pages, "accessibility-coverage"
    if structural_findings > 0 or media_decisions_missing > 0:
        return False, structural_findings + media_decisions_missing, "accessibility-finding"
    if human_tasks_passed < 3:
        return False, human_tasks_passed, "human-evidence"
    assert checked_pages == pages
    return True, human_tasks_passed, "accessible-content-model"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Alternatives model · Theory Media Alternatives
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Media Alternatives: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1708 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Alternatives model · Theory Media Alternatives.
steps.why: Retrieving Media Alternatives through the concrete WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Media Alternatives model · Theory Media Alternatives, keep Media Alternatives connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
